decks.notes: This Mono Blue Control deck focuses on utilizing counterspells and removal to control the game. With powerful cards like Counterspell and Mana Leak, along with versatile threats like Morphling and Mishra's Factory, the deck aims to shut down the opponent's game plan while establishing its own win conditions. Parallax Tide can reset the board if necessary, while cards like Nevinyrral's Disk and Powder Keg provide additional removal options. The deck is designed to outmaneuver and outlast opponents through a combination of control elements and efficient threats.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana and prioritize playing lands in order to power up Terravore as a large threat. The deck also aims to disrupt opponents with cards like Meddling Mage and Armageddon/Cataclysm while maintaining card advantage through Sylvan Library. The aggressive aspect of this deck is further enhanced with creatures like Treetop Village and Call of the Herd, putting pressure on the opponent while also having answers to potential threats with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Naturalize.

The main concept of this Elves aggro deck is to quickly generate mana through cards like Llanowar Elves and Priest of Titania, then use that mana to play a variety of efficient creatures and use Survival of the Fittest to find key cards like Deranged Hermit or Elvish Champion. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of creatures, supported by cards like Wirewood Symbiote and Quirion Ranger for extra utility. Additionally, it includes disruptive elements like Tangle Wire and Nantuko Vigilante to disrupt the opponent's game plan and secure victory.

The main strategy of this deck is to flood the board early with small, efficient white creatures that can quickly overwhelm the opponent. Cards like Mother of Runes and Savannah Lions provide early aggression, while Silver Knight and Soltari Priest offer protection and evasion. The inclusion of removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Cursed Scroll help to maintain board control, while cards like Eternal Dragon and Parallax Wave provide additional utility and staying power. The deck also includes some land disruption with Mishra's Factory and Wasteland to further pressure the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's hand and board through discard spells, removal spells, and hand disruption creatures like Hypnotic Specter. The deck aims to maintain card advantage with cards like Phyrexian Arena and Skeletal Scrying while applying pressure with The Rack and other Specters. The deck also includes some targeted removal spells and ways to disrupt the opponent's strategy, ultimately aiming to control the game and win through attrition.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's resources and controlling the game with cards like Sphere of Resistance and Stone Rain while ramping up with Mox Diamond. The key strategy is to use Oath of Druids to cheat out powerful creatures like Terravore early in the game, overwhelming the opponent with threats. The deck also includes some removal and board wipe spells to deal with threats, as well as land destruction to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck aims to gain a resource advantage and overpower the opponent through efficient control.

The main goal of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures like Multani, Maro-Sorcerer, Akroma, Angel of Wrath, and Reya Dawnbringer using cards like Putrid Imp and Careful Study. Then, the deck utilizes cards like Reanimate and Exhume to bring these creatures back onto the battlefield for a powerful and fast reanimation combo strategy. The deck also includes some control elements with cards like Cabal Therapy and Sickening Dreams to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base includes City of Brass, Gemstone Mine, Underground River, Swamp, and Undiscovered Paradise to support the deck's color requirements.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly swarm the board with small, efficient white creatures and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive attacks. The deck includes a variety of protection spells and removal to ensure your creatures can get through to deal damage. Key cards such as Savannah Lions and Silver Knight provide early pressure, while cards like Paladin en-Vec and Soltari Priest offer additional utility and evasion. The deck also includes a few powerful enchantments like Worship to help maintain board presence and secure the win.

This deck focuses on using a variety of control elements to disrupt the opponent's game plan while also generating card advantage. The main strategy involves using cards like Duress, Cabal Therapy, and Bottomless Pit to disrupt the opponent's hand and resources, while also utilizing The Rack and Cursed Scroll to apply pressure. Additionally, the deck includes some creature threats like Call of the Herd and Rotting Giant to further pressure the opponent. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements while also providing some utility with fetchlands and creature lands.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana through creatures like Llanowar Elves and Priest of Titania to overwhelm opponents with a swarm of Elves. The deck includes several powerful Elf creatures like Elvish Champion and Deranged Hermit to increase the pressure on opponents. The inclusion of cards like Survival of the Fittest allows for efficient creature tutoring and graveyard manipulation, while cards like Tangle Wire and Wasteland provide disruption and control against opposing strategies. Overall, the deck aims to establish a dominant board presence and quickly close out games through aggressive plays.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and efficient spells while utilizing the "madness" mechanic to its fullest potential. By discarding cards with madness costs, such as Arrogant Wurm and Circular Logic, players can maintain a steady stream of threats while also disrupting the opponent's game plan. Survival of the Fittest acts as a powerful tutor to find the necessary creatures, while cards like Wild Mongrel and Basking Rootwalla provide early aggression. The deck aims to control the board and outpace the opponent for a quick victory.

This deck focuses on using cards like Attunement, Replenish, and various enchantments like Opalescence to create a powerful combo that can overwhelm the opponent. The key is to quickly fill the graveyard with enchantments using cards like Frantic Search and Intuition, then use Replenish to bring them all back onto the battlefield for a massive advantage. Cards like Parallax Tide and Parallax Wave help control the board and Seal of Cleansing provides additional removal. The deck aims to set up this combo quickly and efficiently to win the game in a single explosive turn.

The primary focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Replenish, Opalescence, and either Parallax Wave or Parallax Tide to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes various tutors and control cards to protect the combo while developing its mana base. By efficiently cycling through its deck and finding the necessary pieces, the deck aims to win the game through a devastating combo turn that leaves the opponent with few options to respond.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. By playing efficient threats like Grim Lavamancer, Jackal Pup, and Wild Mongrel, along with direct damage spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate, the deck aims to put pressure on the opponent early and finish the game quickly. The inclusion of cards like Call of the Herd and Sylvan Library provide additional card advantage and versatility. This deck seeks to control the board while dealing damage efficiently to secure a victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo of Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle, allowing for a powerful win condition. The deck also includes various control elements such as Counterspell and Foil to protect the combo and disrupt opponent's strategies. Card draw spells like Accumulated Knowledge and Impulse help to find the combo pieces quicker, while cards like Portent and Vision Charm provide card selection and manipulation. The inclusion of Mishra's Factory offers a utility land option for additional pressure. The deck aims to efficiently execute its game plan while maintaining control over the opponent's actions.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling the combo of using Tinker to bring out Phyrexian Devourer and then sacrificing it with Altar of Dementia to mill the opponent's entire library, followed by Fling to deal lethal damage with the Devourer's power. The deck includes lots of fast mana sources and protection in the form of Defense Grid to help set up the combo as quickly as possible. The main goal is to disrupt the opponent's game plan and win the game in a single explosive turn.
